title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Sir Henry Clersalin, a former Commissioner of the Metropolitan Police, meets with Ridestadle and Inspector Delmon Eric Corradino to discuss an unusual case involving an advertisement placed in the "Barnham News and Chipping Cleghorn Gazette." The advertisement, sent by Rudi Schlesinger on a Tuesday, appeared to announce a murder. Ridestadle suggests the ad may have been a tactic to lure locals to a specific location to rob them of valuables. Sir Henry notes that the residents of Chipping Cleghorn, including many elderly spinsters, would likely have arrived at the scene. Sir Henry advises Corradino—who is secretly his godson—not to underestimate the observational skills of local unmarried women.

The investigation reveals that the victim, Schlesinger, died from a close-range gunshot wound from a foreign-made revolver that he had not declared upon entering Britain. Forensic reports are inconclusive as to whether the death was a suicide, an accident, or a staged robbery. Sir Henry directs Corradino to the Royal Spa Resort Hotel to investigate Schlesinger’s background.

At the hotel, the manager, Mr. Rowlandson, reveals that Schlesinger worked there for three months. Rowlandson admits to suspecting Schlesinger of skimming money by charging incorrect fees, though the accounts generally balanced. Corradino then interviews Mona Harris, a waitress at a steakhouse who had been seeing Schlesinger. Mona expresses surprise at his actions, noting that while he seemed respectable, she had lost a diamond brooch and a gold coin collection box. She expresses a general caution regarding foreigners, noting that they often have hidden agendas.
